summary: Monumental The Other One with blazing Dark Star; early jams pristine
blurb: The show erupts with a scorching Dark Star that flows into the iconic Cryptical Envelopment → The Other One suite. Jerry’s guitar is on fire and Phil’s bass interplays brilliantly, while the Caution → Feedback → Good Night sequence caps an intense performance. Recording quality drops after New Potato, but the first half remains crystal clear.
review:
On November 1, 1968 at the Silver Dollar Fairgrounds, the Dead delivered a high‑energy set that still shines despite the inevitable tape woes of the era. The opening Dark Star launches into an adventurous Cryptical Envelopment → The Other One pairing, with Garcia’s surf‑tinged leads and Lesh’s “off‑the‑charts brilliant” bass dialogue creating one of the night’s most memorable moments. After a brief detour through New Potato Caboose and Alligator, the band revs up again for a powerful Caution (Do Not Stop On Tracks) → Feedback → And We Bid You Good Night finale that showcases their improvisational firepower.

While several recordings suffer from drop‑outs and a noticeable dip in sonic clarity after New Potato, the early sections are praised as “crisp” and “sublime,” making this performance a must‑listen for fans of the late ’68 era. The standout tracks—Dark Star, The Other One, and the Caution/Feedback/Good Night suite—remain compelling highlights that outweigh the technical blemishes, especially when heard on the recommended Kaplan soundboard capture.

rating_calculation.sentiment_rationale: Four out of seven recordings are marked positive and three mixed, with consistent praise for The Other One, Caution>Feedback>Good Night, and Jerry’s guitar work. No recurring negative comments about the musical performance were found, so a strong positive adjustment is applied.

best_recording.reason: Early sections are crisp and clear, capturing the peak jams; despite a dip after New Potato, it remains the cleanest overall SBD among the six posted versions.

key_highlights: Monumental The Other One with off‑the‑charts interplay
Caution (Do Not Stop On Tracks) → Feedback → Good Night sequence
Cryptical Envelopment segues into The Other One
song_highlights: And We Bid You Good Night

band_performance.Jerry: Garcia’s guitar is described as awesome, on fire, and surf‑style, delivering blistering leads especially on Dark Star and The Other One; his tone becomes partially buried after New Potato.
band_performance.Phil: Lesh’s bass work shines with creative dialogue and “off‑the‑charts brilliant” interplay throughout the set, particularly during The Other One.
band_performance.Bob: Weir’s rhythm guitar is often buried in the mix, though his brief vocal moment adds a surprise element; no major criticism of his playing noted.

must_listen_sequences: And We Bid You Good Night
